questions.question: A coder mixes $2$ parts of Java with $3$ parts of Python to create a new programming language. What is the ratio of Java to the total mixture?
A gamer has $12$ hours of gameplay data, with $3$ hours of multiplayer and $9$ hours of single-player. What percentage of the data is single-player?
A company mixes $4$ parts of fiber optic cable with $1$ part of copper wire to create a new network cable. What is the ratio of fiber optic cable to copper wire?
A data analyst has a dataset with $15$ columns, $8$ of which are numeric and $7$ of which are categorical. What percentage of the columns are numeric?
A software developer has $18$ hours of coding time, with $6$ hours spent on debugging and $12$ hours spent on writing new code. What is the ratio of debugging time to total coding time?
A tech company has $20$ employees, $12$ of whom are developers and $8$ of whom are designers. What percentage of the employees are designers?
A gamer has a scoreboard with $25$ points, $15$ of which are from multiplayer games and $10$ of which are from single-player games. What is the ratio of single-player points to total points?
A coding challenge has $30$ participants, $18$ of whom are beginners and $12$ of whom are advanced. What percentage of the participants are advanced?

A company mixes $x$ parts of coffee with $y$ parts of tea to create a new beverage. If the ratio of coffee to tea is $3:2$, and the total mixture is $20$ parts, find the values of $x$ and $y$. Show your work and explain your reasoning.

A gamer has $z$ hours of gameplay data, with $p$ hours of multiplayer and $s$ hours of single-player. If the ratio of multiplayer to single-player is $2:3$, and the total gameplay time is $16$ hours, find the values of $p$ and $s$. Show your work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Check for proper units and labeling
Ensure students provide clear explanations for their reasoning
Remind students to show all work and calculations
